---
title: Projects & Students
---

I am always happy to hear from motivated students and researchers interested in working with me. My current research focuses on microbial ecology (dynamical systems/applied PDE), forest-savanna models (mean-field theory/applied PDE), and mathematical epidemiology (within-host malaria models/applied PDE). Please [get in touch](mailto:denis.d.patterson@durham.ac.uk) to discuss potential projects and funding.

---

## Prospective PhD Students

Durham offers a number of PhD funding routes, including the [DDTF](https://www.durham.ac.uk/research/doctoral/ddtf/), [CSC scholarships](https://www.csc.edu.cn/), and other schemes. I am happy to discuss these with interested applicants — please contact me directly.

---

## Undergraduate Summer Research

I have supervised undergraduate researchers at Brandeis, Princeton, and Durham. I am open to discussing summer research opportunities with interested students at Durham. Recent funding has come from the [London Mathematical Society](https://www.lms.ac.uk/) and Durham's [Biophysical Sciences Institute](https://www.durham.ac.uk/research/institutes-and-centres/biophysical-sciences/); applications typically open around January.

### Summer 2025

- **Matthew Black** (Durham, Year 3) — Backward bifurcations in malaria models
  [London Mathematical Society; *next:* PhD student, Cambridge University]
- **Francesca Darkins** (Durham, Year 1) — Ecosystem resilience in a changing climate
  [NERC Research Experience Placement]
- **Aidan Flatt** (Durham, Year 2) — Mathematics of pattern formation
  [Collingwood College bursary]
- **Thomas Mills** (Durham, Year 3) — Mathematical modelling of vegetation dynamics
- **Sarah Mitchell** (Durham, Year 2) — Resilience modelling in medicine and ecology
  [Collingwood College bursary]

### Summer 2024

- **Thomas Shaw** (Durham, Year 3) — Microbial coexistence with resource toxicity
  [Durham Biophysical Sciences Institute; *next:* PhD student, University of Oxford]
- **Ewan Green** (Durham, Year 3) — Mathematical modelling of microbial coexistence
  [London Mathematical Society]

### Princeton & Brandeis

- **Kimberly Shen** (Princeton, Summer 2023 & Senior Thesis) — Fire spread in forest-savanna ecosystems
  [Princeton PACM Certificate Best Research Project Prize; published in *Journal of Mathematical Biology*]
- **Oliver Liang** (Brandeis, Fall 2021) — Collective dynamics of mobile particles
- **Jingman Li & Yuning Liu** (Brandeis, Fall 2020) — Network epidemic models
- **Hange Zhu** (Brandeis, Spring 2020) — Pattern formation in heterogeneous domains
- **Hanyu Song** (Brandeis, Summer 2019) — Mathematical models of somitogenesis

---

## Durham Final Year Projects

- The Mathematics of Evolution *(4th year, 2025/2026)*
- Numerical Bifurcation Analysis of Complex Systems *(4th year, 2024/2025)*
- Forced Nonlinear Systems *(3rd year, 2024/2025)*
